subject: DevOps
title: CrowdStrike's Global Blue Screen (2024)
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on CrowdStrike's Global Blue Screen (2024).

hook.text: July 19, 2024. 8.5 million Windows PCs crashed at once. Airlines, banks, hospitals — all down.

buildup.text: CrowdStrike pushed a faulty sensor config update. It caused a kernel-level crash on boot.

discovery.text: The update skipped staged rollout. Every Windows machine with the agent got it at once.

twist.text: Machines couldn't boot. The fix required manual intervention on each individual computer.

climax.text: Airlines grounded flights. Hospitals canceled surgeries. The estimated cost: over $10 billion.

punchline.text: Canary your updates. Especially kernel-level ones.

quiz.question: Why was the CrowdStrike incident so devastating?
options: The update was pushed to all machines at once without staged rollout
The software was outdated and unpatched
